questions.prompt: What's the only correct Cloudflare SSL mode for production?

choices.text: Full (strict). TLS on both legs (user↔Cloudflare and Cloudflare↔origin), with cert validation at origin. Other modes either skip encryption on Cloudflare↔origin (Flexible — never use) or skip cert validation (Full — accepts self-signed). Only Full (strict) actually delivers end-to-end TLS.
Off — encryption isn't worth the latency.
Full — same as strict for most purposes.

questions.explanation: Full (strict) is the only mode that's actually end-to-end TLS. Flexible is the security anti-pattern (looks encrypted to users; isn't on the back leg). Full without strict accepts self-signed origin certs and doesn't prevent MITM between Cloudflare and origin.

questions.prompt: Your origin needs a cert for Cloudflare to validate (Full strict mode). What's the simplest option that needs no renewal management?

choices.text: Cloudflare Origin Certificate — free, long-lived (up to 15 years), issued by Cloudflare specifically for Cloudflare↔origin traffic. Cloudflare's edge trusts it; browsers don't (by design — origin certs aren't for direct browser access). Install at origin, set Full (strict), done.

questions.explanation: Cloudflare Origin Certs are the canonical pattern: free, long-lived, zero renewal hassle, only valid for Cloudflare-to-origin traffic. Generate in dashboard, install at origin (NGINX/Apache/hosting platform), enable Full (strict). Public Let's Encrypt certs also work but require renewal automation.
